title: rubus fruticosus subsp. affinis
intro: Rubus fruticosus subsp. affinis, also known as the Himalayan blackberry, is a subspecies of the bramble plant that is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus subsp. affinis include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant protection against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
